authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service (Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest, Carson-Iceberg Wilderness)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(100) — Murray Canyon Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.)
sources.quotedText: (100) Murray Canyon Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) upstream from the falls located about 1/4 mile above the confluence with the East Fork Carson River. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (updated June 1, 2026)
Murray Canyon Trail | Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest | Forest Service
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Closed water begins upstream from a falls located about 1/4 mile above Murray Canyon Creek's confluence with the East Fork Carson River. Murray Canyon Creek is a small tributary of the East Fork Carson River, within the Carson-Iceberg Wilderness (Humboldt-Toiyabe National Forest) south of Markleeville, a drainage within the historic native range of Lahontan cutthroat trout; closures above natural barrier falls are commonly used in this basin to protect isolated native/refuge trout populations from downstream hybridization, though CDFW's regulation text does not itself state the specific management reason for this closure.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year upstream from the falls about 1/4 mile above the confluence with the East Fork Carson River
regulations.verbatimText: Murray Canyon Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) upstream from the falls located about 1/4 mile above the confluence with the East Fork Carson River. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. (Note: this water is closed to all fishing all year; license requirement is noted for statewide consistency only.)

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
